commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Delica Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Delica's 4D56 and 4M40 diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with turbo wear, injector leaks and timing belt failures most common. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Delica.

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why 4D56/4M40 Engines Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil seal wear allows oil into intercooler, reducing boost and causing blue smoke; often accelerated by infrequent oil changes on high-mileage grey imports.

repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if housing undamaged; 20k-40k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit refurbished turbo unit with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable if oil system flushed; 40k-70k mile expectation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4D56/4M40 with upgraded turbo
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; 12-month warranty; best for >100k mile engines

problems.recommendation: On a Delica worth £3,000-£8,000, a standalone turbo rebuild offers good value if caught early. However, if the engine has exceeded 100k miles or shows other wear signs, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo often proves more cost-effective long-term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4D56 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Delica

problems.h4: Injector Seal Leakage - Hard Starting & Rough Running
problems.rootCause: Copper injector washers and seals harden with age, allowing compression leak-back; causes difficult cold starts, white smoke and fuel odour in cabin.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace injector copper washers and seals
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if injectors themselves are sound; 30k-50k mile fix

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, test and refurbish all injectors on bench
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses worn nozzles; recommended if mileage >110k

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new injectors
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if multiple components worn; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: Seal replacement is a cost-effective first step on lower-mileage Delicas. If injectors show wear on testing or the engine has other issues, moving straight to a rebuilt unit with new injectors often saves money versus staged repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Delica injector repair or engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Interference Damage
problems.rootCause: All Delica diesel and V6 petrol engines are interference design; belt failure causes pistons to strike valves, bending valves and potentially damaging pistons/cylinder head.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump
Supply & fit rebuilt engine after belt failure damage
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable option post-failure; includes upgraded components

problems.recommendation: Never gamble with timing belt intervals on a Delica. If the belt has failed, repair is rarely economical - a rebuilt engine with new belt kit fitted offers far better value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Delica rebuilt engine prices after timing belt failure

problems.h4: DPF Regeneration Failure - D:5 4N14 Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Delica D:5 (2007-2019, 4N14 common rail diesel)
problems.rootCause: Short journey driving prevents active DPF regeneration; soot accumulation triggers limp mode, and forced regeneration attempts can damage turbo or EGR components.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-vehicle chemical cleaning of DPF substrate
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if no structural damage; requires post-clean coding

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned DPF unit with sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Costly; only recommended if engine otherwise healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4N14 with DPF delete option
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage D:5; *check emissions compliance

problems.recommendation: For D:5 Delicas used primarily on short trips, DPF cleaning may restore function temporarily. However, if the engine has other wear or high mileage, a rebuilt 4N14 with specialist DPF management advice often proves more reliable long-term.
problems.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Delica D:5 4N14 engine replacement options

problems.h4: Overheating & Head Gasket Failure - Coolant Loss Warning
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Variable; often triggered by cooling system neglect
problems.rootCause: Radiator blockage, failed thermostat or water pump causes overheating; aluminium cylinder heads warp, compromising head gasket seal and allowing coolant/oil mixing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Flush system, replace thermostat, water pump, hoses
Remove head, machine surface, fit new gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/head undamaged; compression test essential first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating was severe or prolonged

problems.recommendation: If your Delica has overheated, get a compression test before deciding. Minor overheating may only need cooling system work; severe overheating often warrants a rebuilt engine to avoid repeat failure.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Delica engine replacement after overheating

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Delica
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Delica suffers turbo wear, injector leaks or timing belt damage, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Delica specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Delica Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Delica and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

fuelTypes.h2: Mitsubishi Delica Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Mitsubishi Delica engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early L300 diesel to the latest D:5 common rail.

items.title: Mitsubishi Delica Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominates the Delica range across L300, L400 and D:5 generations, with 4D56, 4M40 and 4N14 codes covering most UK grey import replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominates the Delica range across L300, L400 and D:5 generations, with 4D56, 4M40 and 4N14 codes covering most UK grey import replacement enquiries.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi Delica Diesel Engine Families
families: 4D56 - L300/L400 workhorse, turbo variants popular for camper conversions
4M40 - L400 high-torque option, electronic injection on later models
4N14 - D:5 common rail, DPF-equipped, requires specialist coding
foundIn: D:5 (2007-2019) - 4N14 common rail with DPF and EGR
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for camper van towing
Turbocharger wear common on high-mileage grey imports
Timing belt critical maintenance (interference design)
DPF regeneration issues on D:5 with short-journey use
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Delica Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Delica Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Delicas are rarer in the UK grey import market but offer simpler emissions compliance and smoother refinement for non-towing applications.
items.descriptor: Petrol Delicas are rarer in the UK grey import market but offer simpler emissions compliance and smoother refinement for non-towing applications.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi Delica Petrol Engine Families
families: 4G64 - L300 SOHC petrol, simple and reliable but less economical
6G72 - L400 V6 petrol, smooth power for Super Exceed variants
6B31 - D:5 modern V6, rare but powerful for performance builds
foundIn: L400 (1994-2007) - 6G72 3.0L V6 petrol in Super Exceed
knownFor: Simpler fuel system than diesel, easier DIY maintenance
Timing belt critical (all interference engines)
Lower torque than diesel, less ideal for heavy camper loads
Better cold-start reliability in UK winters
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Delica Petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Delica Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: No factory hybrid, plug-in hybrid or electric Mitsubishi Delica variants were officially sold in the UK market; all replacements are conventional combustion engines.
items.descriptor: No factory hybrid, plug-in hybrid or electric Mitsubishi Delica variants were officially sold in the UK market; all replacements are conventional combustion engines.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi Delica Hybrid / Electrified Engine Families
importantNotes: No MHEV, PHEV, HEV or BEV Delica models exist for UK grey imports
Aftermarket camper conversions may add auxiliary battery systems
Engine replacement focuses on original diesel or petrol units only

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Delica Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Delica's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original L300 Delica introduced the versatile 4D56 diesel platform that would dominate UK grey import demand for decades.
keyChanges: L300 platform launched with SWB and LWB body options
4D56 diesel introduced in naturally aspirated and turbo variants
4G64 petrol option available for non-turbo applications
First wave of Japanese grey imports to UK begins
knownFor: Simple, robust mechanical design favoured by camper converters
Timing belt critical maintenance (interference engine)
Turbo variants prone to seal wear at high mileage
Strong parts availability via Japanese import channels
years.cta: Get quotes for 1986-1994 Mitsubishi Delica replacement engines
years.ticker: L300 4D56 engine replacement • L300 turbo failure fix • L300 timing belt cost

years.description: The L400 generation refined the Delica formula with updated styling, 4M40 diesel option and electronic injection on later models.
keyChanges: L400 platform launched with improved crash structure and refinement
Electronic injection (4M40-TE) added on Exceed/Super Exceed trims
High roof and camper-ready Chamonix trim variants popularised
knownFor: L400 most searched Delica generation in UK due to camper popularity
4M40 offers stronger torque for heavy conversions
Electronic injection variants require specialist diagnostics
V6 petrol rare but sought for performance camper builds
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-2007 Mitsubishi Delica replacement engines
years.ticker: L400 4M40 engine replacement • Chamonix turbo upgrade • L400 injector seal leak fix

years.description: The D:5 introduced modern common rail diesel (4N14) with DPF and advanced electronics, requiring specialist coding for replacement.
keyChanges: D:5 platform launched with car-like refinement and safety tech
4N14 common rail diesel replaced older 4D56/4M40 units
DPF and EGR emissions systems added, affecting replacement strategy
Petrol V6 (6B31) option continued for non-diesel markets
mainEngines: 4N14 - 2.2L common rail diesel with DPF (D:5 primary)
knownFor: 4N14 delivers strong economy but DPF issues on short journeys
Specialist diagnostics essential for ECU coding post-replacement
Higher rebuild costs reflect complex common rail technology
Rare in UK; specialist suppliers command premium pricing

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Delica Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Delica engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Mitsubishi Delica engine replacement costs vary by engine type and condition. Used engines start from £950, reconditioned units from £1,850, and rebuilt engines from £2,100, all supply-only. Supply & fit adds £450-£850 depending on location and complexity.
keyPoints: Used 4D56 engines: £950-£1,650 supply only
Reconditioned units include new turbo and injectors
Rebuilt engines offer longest lifespan with warranty
Supply & fit service available nationwide via network
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Delica engine prices from verified UK suppliers

items.question: What engine code is in my Mitsubishi Delica?
items.answer: Most UK Mitsubishi Delica L300 and L400 models use the 4D56 2.5L turbo diesel engine. L400 Exceed/Super Exceed may have the 4M40 2.8L diesel, while D:5 models use the 4N14 common rail diesel. Enter your registration to confirm your exact code.
keyPoints: 4D56: L300/L400 2.5L diesel, most common in UK
4M40: L400 2.8L diesel, higher torque option
4N14: D:5 2.2L common rail, requires specialist coding
DVLA lookup instantly verifies your engine code
items.cta: Use our registration checker to identify your Delica engine code

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Delica 4D56 engine reliable?
items.answer: The 4D56 is generally robust when maintained, but turbo seals, injectors and timing belts commonly fail on high-mileage grey imports. Regular oil changes and timely belt replacement significantly extend lifespan.
keyPoints: Simple SOHC design aids repairability and parts availability
Turbo failure common after 80k miles without maintenance
Timing belt must be changed every 60k miles (interference engine)
Rebuilt units with upgraded components offer best long-term value
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 4D56 Mitsubishi Delica engines with warranty

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Mitsubishi Delica?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but complex due to ECU, wiring and mounting differences. Sticking with the original 4D56/4M40/4N14 code ensures compatibility, emissions compliance and avoids costly fabrication work.
keyPoints: 4D56 and 4M40 share similar mounting points on L400
ECU and immobiliser coding required for any swap
Non-original engines may fail UK IVA/emissions checks
Specialist advice essential before attempting conversion
items.cta: Compare compatible Mitsubishi Delica engine replacement options

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi Delica engine last?
items.answer: Well-maintained 4D56/4M40 engines can exceed 180,000 miles, but grey import history and maintenance records vary. Timing belt changes every 60k miles and quality oil are critical to reaching high mileage.
keyPoints: 180k+ miles achievable with full service history
Turbo and injector wear accelerate without quality oil
Timing belt failure destroys engine (interference design)
Rebuilt engines reset the mileage clock with warranty
items.cta: Find low-mileage used Mitsubishi Delica engines with compression test reports

items.question: Do I need to replace the gearbox when replacing my Delica engine?
items.answer: No, engine and gearbox are separate units. However, specify manual or automatic when ordering, as bellhousing patterns and ECU compatibility differ. Most suppliers can match your existing transmission type.
keyPoints: Manual and automatic 4D56 have different bellhousings
ECU may need recoding for automatic variants
Clutch/flywheel often replaced during engine swap
Confirm transmission type when requesting quotes
items.cta: Get Mitsubishi Delica engine quotes matched to your transmission

items.question: Will a Japanese spec Mitsubishi Delica engine fit my UK import?
items.answer: Yes, engines from Japanese right-hand drive Delicas are direct fit for UK grey imports. Ensure the engine code, transmission type and ancillary layout match your vehicle for seamless installation.
keyPoints: JDM engines sourced from same RHD chassis as UK imports
Verify engine code (4D56/4M40/4N14) matches original
Ancillary brackets may differ between trim levels
Full import documentation provided with all engines
items.cta: Compare Japanese spec Mitsubishi Delica engines with UK compliance docs

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Mitsubishi Delica engine?
items.answer: Minimum 6-month warranty on used engines, 12-month unlimited mileage on reconditioned and rebuilt units from EngineMarket suppliers. Always confirm exact terms, coverage exclusions and claim process with your chosen supplier.
keyPoints: Used engines: 6-month minimum parts warranty
Reconditioned/rebuilt: 12-month unlimited mileage standard
Warranty valid only when fitted by approved specialist (supply & fit)
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: View Mitsubishi Delica engine options with 12-month warranty included

items.question: How do I know which Mitsubishi Delica engine I need?
items.answer: Enter your registration number for instant DVLA verification of your exact model, engine code and fuel type. Alternatively, check the engine identification plate near the injection pump or consult your import documentation.
keyPoints: DVLA lookup confirms variant, engine code and year instantly
Engine code stamped on block near injection pump
Import paperwork lists original engine specifications
Our compatibility guarantee ensures correct fitment
items.cta: Use our VIN decoder to confirm your Delica engine requirements

items.question: Can I get supply and fit for my Mitsubishi Delica engine?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's nationwide network offers supply & fit service for Mitsubishi Delica engines. This includes engine delivery, professional installation, ECU coding, ancillary transfer and post-fit testing with warranty activation.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £450-£850 to engine price
Network specialists experienced with grey import Delicas
ECU coding and immobiliser matching included
Warranty only valid when fitted by approved specialist
items.cta: Book Mitsubishi Delica engine supply & fit with nationwide coverage
